Are you having a real passion for winning? Are you intrigued by the idea to build long-term business relationships with our customers? To develop and negotiate plans which create a win-win for our shoppers, our customers and our Company? Do you have an ambitious spirit and a willingness for learning?

As a Key Account Manager, you´ll directly take meaningful and impacting responsibilities as of day 1.
You´ll be in charge for maintaining and expanding a long-term partnership with your customers by continuously proposing solutions and plans that meet their objectives. To do so, you’ll develop a keen understanding of our product categories and markets.

• Recognized state of the art marketing skills – We will constantly help you improve your marketing knowledge and management abilities.
• Continuous coaching & mentorship– We are passionate about our work. We will make sure you receive both formal training and regular mentorship from your manager and others.

# ========================= Row 5 ======.title: Marketing Manager
# ========================= Row 5 ======.job_description:
No Relocation Assistance Offered
Job Number #171267 - Hong Kong, Hong Kong, China
Who We Are
Colgate-Palmolive Company is a global consumer products company operating in over 200 countries specializing in Oral Care, Personal Care, Home Care, Skin Care, and Pet Nutrition. Our products are trusted in more households than any other brand in the world, making us a household name!
Join Colgate-Palmolive, a caring, innovative growth company reimagining a healthier future for people, their pets, and our planet. Guided by our core values—Caring, Inclusive, and Courageous—we foster a culture that inspires our people to achieve common goals. Together, let's build a brighter, healthier future for all.

Marketing  Manager - Manual Toothbrush
Enterprise Oral Care - Manual Toothbrush
Colgate-Palmolive is a leading consumer goods company that serves global customers with products in four major categories: oral care, personal care, home care, and pet nutrition.  Along with Colgate toothpaste,
the highest penetrated brand in the world, we connect our products to consumers in over 200 countries
under internationally recognized brand names such as Colgate, Palmolive, Softsoap, Irish Spring, Protex,
Toms of Maine, Hill's pet nutrition, etc. Like most CPG’s, eCommerce is at the forefront of our companies’ growth agenda and is a key business priority globally.
Role & Responsibilities
How does this role fit in? The opportunity exists to be the Marketing Manager for manual toothbrushes. As a part of the Global Enterprise Oral Care team, this role is expected to collaborate with cross functional teams and top markets all around the world to provide on-going strategies and 3 year innovation pipeline for the specific part of the portfolio - while the global headquarter is New York based, this role will be physically based in Hong Kong, sitting in close proximity with the APAC divisional team due to the role’s heavy focus on the APAC markets especially China and India.
Key responsibilities:
Set the innovation & renovation roadmap of toothbrush brands/sub-brands through development and execution of the category strategy (manual toothbrushes)
Develop a deep understanding of global strategy. Use this understanding to address consumer needs at a division & country level
Utilize financial acumen to take key business decisions that are  profitable, increase global equities and offer clear value-added benefits to consumers
Gain credibility and trust through Sub/Division/Global relationships and build commitment to common goal
Initiate the development and testing of product alternatives and improvements that address consumer need gaps
Develop product development plans based on category financial needs, markets, and applicable time frames
Stay abreast of CP and competitor activity (new launches & relaunches),  both successes and failures
Leverage analytics to draw quantitative insights - for CP & competitors. Initiate action when required based in competitor activity
Leverage social listening and digital tools to trendspot the latest in the world of innovation
What you can expect at Colgate-Palmolive:
● Be a part of living a culture of agility, growth and innovation
● Learn how a large business operates and grow in your overall business knowledge and acumen
o You will be exposed to  learnings on a global scale that will challenge and stretch your strategic and analytical capabilities
● Work with great colleagues. As people at Colgate will tell you it’s the people that make the
difference. You will establish great personal networks and work across a multitude of cultures
What we ask.
You bring knowledge, passion, curiosity and a desire to change the (Colgate) world. Exhibit and live the growth and challenger mindset
You bring an informed, educated perspective on brand and portfolio strategy and a high degree of intellectual curiosity and humility to help the company accelerate our growth
5 years of marketing experience with increasing levels of responsibility
A proven track record of performance in brand management including consumer insights, driving innovation, and delivering the brand P&L
You have a strong business sense, analytical skills, and the ability to crystallize multiple data points into clear insight to develop the business case for new innovation
Communication is your forte and you lead with the ability to clearly articulate key business concepts and communicate complex technical ideas and results to non-technical partners
You have experience working with cross-cultural and geographically dispersed multi functional teams and agency partners
You have the expertise and experience in China & India markets especially on Toothbrush
Knowledge of written and spoken Mandarin will be an added skill for the role

# ========================= Row 7 ======.title: Assistant Accounting Manager
# ========================= Row 7 ======.job_description:
We are Reckitt
 
Home to the world's best loved and trusted hygiene, health, and nutrition brands. Our purpose defines why we exist: to protect, heal and nurture in the relentless pursuit of a cleaner, healthier world. We are a global team united by this purpose.
Join us in our fight to make access to the highest quality hygiene, wellness, and nourishment a right and not a privilege.
​Finance​
 
Our Finance team is not just another department; we're strategic partners and catalysts for change, who use our expertise to drive sustainable growth and create and protect value for Reckitt.
With a strong emphasis on cross-functional collaboration and partnership, our inclusive, diverse and energised team breaks down traditional barriers, enabling you to thrive in a dynamic environment where you and your ideas matter.
From leveraging insights and analysis to make data-driven decisions - to spearheading sustainable business practices that contribute to consistent and reliable business growth - we are unwaveringly passionate about making a meaningful impact, and always doing the right thing.
About the role
 
Are you ready to dive into numbers and drive success? Reckitt is on the lookout for a sharp Finance Manager Commercial who thrives in a fast-paced environment and is passionate about shaping financial strategies. This is a stellar opportunity to emerge as a financial champion, analysing data to fuel profit growth and taking the reins of our economic health. With your insights, we'll streamline costs, bolster financial fortitude, and map out a prosperous future. If you're eager to see your financial acumen manifest into tangible success, we'd love to welcome you aboard.
Your responsibilities
 
- Cover GL for HK market.
- Assess financial reports and trends, making sense of our fiscal position. 
- Craft and execute initiatives to curb unnecessary expenditures, enhancing efficiency. 
- Deploy your expertise with financial tools to generate detailed reports, steering us towards data-driven decisions. 
- Foster collaborative efforts to instil cost-conscious practices across the company. 
- Navigate financial landscapes to ensure Reckitt's robust financial standing and strategic growth.
The experience we're looking for
 
- Credentialed accountant with a penchant for number crunching.

- A track record of conjuring up detailed financial analysis and reporting.

- Safeguard the company's assets and integrity with adept risk management and internal controls.

- Keen influencer with the prowess to guide stakeholders through complex financial landscapes.

- A wizard in budgeting and forecasting, preparing for tomorrow's climate today.

- Proficient in manoeuvring through financial software with ease, bringing data to life.

- Autonomously implements projects, yet flourishes in a team, nurturing relationships that lead to shared victories.
The skills for success
 
Business accumen, Investment Analysis, Budgeting & Forecasting, Project Management, Improve business processes, Accounting principles, Financial Reporting, Internal controls, Financial analysis and reporting, Stakeholder engagement and influence, Strategic thinking, Risk management, Relationship management.
What we offer
 
With inclusion at the heart of everything we do, working alongside our four global Employee Resource Groups, we support our people at every step of their career journey, helping them to succeed in their own individual way. We invest in the wellbeing of our people through parental benefits, an Employee Assistance Program to promote mental health, and life insurance for all employees globally. We have a range of other benefits in line with the local market. Through our global share plans we offer the opportunity to save and share in Reckitt's potential future successes. For eligible roles, we also offer short-term incentives to recognise, appreciate and reward your work for delivering outstanding results. You will be rewarded in line with Reckitt's pay for performance philosophy.
Equality
 
We recognise that in real life, great people don't always 'tick all the boxes'. That's why we hire for potential as well as experience. Even if you don't meet every point on the job description, if this role and our company feels like a good fit for you, we still want to hear from you.

All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to age, disability or medical condition; colour, ethnicity, race, citizenship, and national origin; religion, faith; pregnancy, family status and caring responsibilities; sexual orientation; sex, gender identity, gender expression, and transgender identity; protected veteran status; size or any other basis protected by appropriate law.

# ========================= Row 8 ======.title: Retail Supervisor
# ========================= Row 8 ======.location: Hong Kong Special Administrati, HK, HK, 999077
# ========================= Row 8 ======.job_description:
Purpose of the role:
Act as the key driving force for business development and management for Prestige Retail & Wholesale Sector.
Oversee daily operations for Prestige Retail Sector, from managing staff, inventory, operational flows to ensuring retail and operation excellence.
Support Business Development Manager/ Retail Manager to create and achieve sales goals by driving excellent activation and ensuring fulfilment.
In all circumference, to mmaintain high quality of customer experience and retail excellence to drive both offtake sales and sell in revenue.
RESPONSIBILITIES
Your main focus:
Business Planning and Management
Support Business Development Manager/ Retail Manager in developing business strategies and identifying business potential with guidance to convert the vision into achievable goals.
Establish sales objectives by forecasting and developing monthly/ yearly sales targets and KPIs for wholesale customer and front line workforce.
Drive ambitious sales volume with company strategic product mix in accordance with brand directions.
Understand the competitive landscape and market trends to modify business action to react the changing retailing world.
Resourcing optimization to protect company profit on each investment.
Achieve Company Goals and Target
Drive & achieve the successful implement of brand activities with marketing team to maximize traffic, conversion, customer recruitment and sales.
Track, analysis and communicate KPIs to ensure delivery of sales target.
Provide key business insights and propose timely action to ensure sales delivery.
Drive Retail Excellence
Develop holistic customer journey and selling ceremony to perform Best-In-Class shopping experience in the market.
Strengthen front line workforce mindset on driving customer satisfaction and excellent service.
Build up customer loyalty with effective CRM program, by understanding customer purchasing behaviour and preference across different brands, channels and doors.
Closely monitoring on team performance to deliver consistent service standard, by mystery shopping program, role play, coaching etc.
Team Development & Performance
Cultivate team with strong fighting spirit, positive and Best-In-Class mindset.
Build up a high effective team by designing team structure, recruiting, developing and retaining talent to maximize their productivity.
Collaborate with HR on recruitment, talent management, compensation direction and disciplinary policy.
Identify development focus of team and individual workforce, then to work with Training & Education Team to set training priority and implement training roadmap.
Formulate system on performance checking of workforce and evaluate effectiveness of each people development action.
Propose commission and incentive scheme based on Reward-On-KPI mindset, to elevate team performance, as well as to maintain competitive package compared beauty industry.
Operational Management
Maintain effective and compliance operation flows.
Collaborate with all stakeholders, to improve operational outcome and simplify the procedure.
Asset management at front line area to protect company interests, including stock and material control.
Key Account Management
Be a Coty Ambassador to introduce brand and product direction to key clients.
Manage and nurture business relationship with key clients, by understanding business needs, anticipating challenges and providing strategic business plan.
Act as a bridge between Coty and key clients on leading projects and aligning business plan with internal stakeholders.
Be a strategic mastermind behind crafting brand activation plans from key clients.
Forecast and monitor sales trend to ensure delivering revenue and profitability to Coty.
Work with cross functional teams to meet deadlines/ lead time required for promotional packs, price changes arrangement, new product launch, POSM and others to ensure on-time and effective implementation.
Plan and execute sales promotions, new product listings, in-store activities, and marketing events in collaboration with key accounts
Closely monitor product launches, execution and sell-in /sell-through results and reports as in monthly basis.
YOU ARE A COTY FIT
As an experienced role, you get energy from working in a fast-paced, diverse and international environment. Other than that, you:
Bachelor’s degree in business management and/or Marketing (or related/equivalent).
Minimum of 3 years’ experience of sales, retail or marketing role in FMCG/ Retailing/ Wholesale Industry, experience in Beauty or Luxury Product industries is added benefit.
Previous experience of retail operation management.
Result oriented with strong ability to drive team performance and inspire growth in a fast-paced and challenging environment.
Energetic, well-organized, presentable, detail-minded, logical thinking, able to work independently
Able to work under pressure and tight schedule
Customer-centric and have a mindset of attention to details and quality.
Strong business acumen and solid analytical skills.
Excellent leadership, interpersonal, communication and presentation skills.
Excellent spoken and written communication skills of English, Cantonese and Mandarin.
Competent with Microsoft Office.
Travel is needed but not frequent.
OUR BENEFITS
As our this role, some of the benefits you will receive are:
A salary that matches your knowledge and experience
Great holiday benefits
Comprehensive medical and commercial insurance
Fair rewards given based on performance and individual abilities
RECRUITMENT PROCESS
Our recruiter will contact you within 10 working days.
A telephone/online introductory meeting follows.
A first online/in-person interview
A second interview
You will receive a proposal with the terms of employment.

# ========================= Row 12 =====.title: Assistant Sales Manager
# ========================= Row 12 =====.job_description:
This position is responsible for developing modern on business and network; and building up strong and long-term relationship with key cocktail bars, restaurant outlets, luxury hotels & membership clubs, through quality service and professional advice tailored to their needs. 

 The incumbent is accountable to plan , implement and co-ordinate all sales strategies and programs in order to achieve sales target and business growth . 

 Key Responsibilities and Job-Related Characteristics: 
 Responsible for achieving sales targets, distribution target and merchandising/visibility objectives in the designated channel.
 Work with Channel Manager to recommend annual sales and promotion plans for Modern on-trade channel.
 Actively seek new business opportunities to increase profits and channel penetration of PR products.
 Ensure all pouring contracts are reviewed and renewed in a timely manner.
 Work with Marketing Department to implement promotion activities and provide constructive feedback on promotion effectiveness and make recommendations where necessary.
 Collect & analyze market intelligence and report on competitors’ activities.
 Secure & maximize product display and brand visibility in all signature outlets.
 Develop and maintain good customer relationship with professional service, and be the preferred business partner of our customers
 Carry out sales administrative role in due course
 Ensure payment collection is in line with channel and company objectives.

 Knowledge and Skills Requirements: 
 Degree holder, with 3-5 years relevant experience in trading field (alcoholic industry or FMCG is preferred)
 Experience in modern on-trade outlets/passionate in Food & Beverage industry is preferred
 Dynamic, outgoing with good initiative and interpersonal skills
 Proactive, with self-motivated mindset to drive result and achieve target
 Good interpersonal, communication and negotiation skills
 Strong numeric sense with good analytical skills
 Proficient in both written and spoken English
 Proficient in MS Office (Excel, Word, Powerpoint)
 Willing to work late hours
 Candidate with less experience may be considered as Assistant Sales Manager

 Target Hire Date : 
2026-03-01

# ========================= Row 13 =====.title: Assistant Manager, Human Resources
# ========================= Row 13 =====.job_description:
1. HR Business Partner Support 
 Act as a proactive and credible HR partner, aligning HR strategy with evolving business priorities and market dynamics. 
 Assist line manager in providing HR advisory support to business leaders, including organizational design, workforce planning, and performance management. 
 Implement of HR strategies and initiatives that align with business goals. 
 Analyze people data (e.g., turnover, engagement, promotion rates) to diagnose root causes, recommend evidence-based interventions, and measure impact on retention and productivity. 

 2. Employee Lifecycle Management 
 Independently manage the entire employee lifecycle, including onboarding, probation, transfers, promotions, and offboarding by collaborating with share service team, ensuring compliance with local policies and regulations. 
 Work with HRSSC team to maintain accurate employee data and support system-related projects and updates. 

 3. Talent Acquisition and Development 
 Manage talent acquisition process to attract right talents 
 Coordinate talent development programs with COEs, including training needs analysis, program delivery, and evaluation. 
 Support the execution of talent management processes such as high-potential identification, career pathing, and development planning. 

 4. Employee Engagement & Culture 
 Lead the planning and execution of employee engagement activities (e.g., festive events, wellness programs, team-building) to foster a positive workplace culture. 
 Support the rollout of diversity, equity, and inclusion initiatives. 
 Analyze engagement data and propose actionable insights to enhance employee satisfaction and retention. 

 5. HR Operations  
 Work together with line manager on HR budgeting, forecasting, and cost-tracking activities. 
 Collaborate with HR shared services, C&B, and finance teams to ensure smooth HR operations.

# ========================= Row 13 =====.title: Brand Manager
# ========================= Row 13 =====.job_description:
Key Responsibilities and Job-Related Characteristics: 

 Brand Strategy & Planning: 
 Develop and implement annual brand plans for Martell and The Chuan, aligned with global strategies.
 Lead market-specific initiatives for Macau, tailoring brand activations to local consumer insights and market dynamics.
 Monitor brand performance, track market trends, and conduct competitive analysis to identify growth opportunities.

 Marketing Campaigns & Activations: 
 Create and execute integrated 360° marketing campaigns, including digital, social media, PR, events, and in-trade activations.
 Collaborate with agencies and internal teams to produce high-quality creative content and consumer engagements.
 Plan and manage brand events, launches, and experiences that elevate brand equity and drive trial.

 Commercial & Trade Development: 
 Work closely with the trade marketing and sales team to develop marketing programs, promotional activities, and channel strategies.
 Ensure effective brand visibility and excellence in execution across key accounts, including luxury hotels, fine dining, bars, and retail.
 Support new product launches and drive sell-in strategies with trade partners.

 Budget Management: 
 Manage brand budgets efficiently, ensuring optimal allocation of resources and ROI on marketing investments.
 Monitor expenditures, track campaign performance, and provide regular reports to senior management.

 Stakeholder Collaboration: 
 Partner with global brand teams to leverage best practices, align on brand guidelines, and secure support for local initiatives.
 Collaborate with cross-functional teams including Sales, Finance, Supply Chain, and Digital to ensure seamless execution.

 Qualifications & Experience 
 Bachelor’s degree in Marketing, Business, or related field; MBA is a plus.
 Minimum 5 years of brand management experience, preferably in the luxury spirits, FMCG, or premium consumer goods industry.
 Proven track record in developing and executing successful marketing campaigns in Hong Kong and/or Macau markets.
 Strong understanding of the trade landscape, consumer behaviour, and digital marketing trends in Greater China.
 Excellent project management, analytical, and communication skills.
 Fluent in English and Chinese (Cantonese and Mandarin); proficiency in Portuguese is a plus for Macau market.
 Creative thinker with a passion for brand building and consumer engagement.

# ========================= Row 13 =====.title: Private Client Manager
# ========================= Row 13 =====.job_description:
Key Responsibilities 1.Strategy & Business Development
 Develop and execute a market-specific sales strategy to recruit, engage, and grow (U)HNWI and VIP clients.
 Identify new revenue opportunities through business development, partnerships, and luxury ecosystem activation.
 Recommend portfolio and brand initiatives aligned with pricing strategy, margin expectations, and resource optimisation.
 Actively contribute to building the Private Client business structure in an entrepreneurial, fast‑evolving environment.

 2.Client Recruitment & Relationship Building
 Recruit, nurture, and retain personal relationships with (U)HNWIs through high-touch service and personalised interactions.
 Develop a robust pipeline of prospects through luxury partnerships, connector networks, private events, and targeted engagement.
 Strengthen long-term relationships to maximise client value, repeat purchases, and premium brand advocacy.

 3.Sales Performance & Commercial Execution
 Deliver annual D2C revenue, margin, and profitability targets for the Prestige and Prestige+ portfolio.
 Ensure superior execution of client orders, premium delivery experience, and after‑sales service.
 Propose commercial solutions aligned with agreed pricing, profitability thresholds, inventory constraints, and commercial KPIs.
 Monitor client segmentation, spending patterns, and CAPP performance to optimise commercial outcomes.

 4.Luxury Brand Representation & Experience Creation
 Serve as a charismatic and credible brand ambassador, confidently representing Pernod Ricard’s finest Maisons.
 Host private tastings, prestige events, and bespoke experiences that showcase craftsmanship, heritage, and brand storytelling.
 Design personalised, unforgettable experiences that reflect luxury standards and strengthen emotional connection with the brand.

 5.Reporting, CRM Excellence & Compliance
 Maintain accurate and up-to-date CRM and client records with full compliance to internal controls.
 Provide regular reporting on client activities, insights, sales pipeline, and market trends.
 Capture and share HNWI insights to influence product development, experience design, and strategic initiatives.

 6.Market Intelligence & Innovation
 Monitor luxury trends, competitor movements, and shifts in HNWI behaviour to anticipate market needs.
 Recommend and test innovative engagement strategies, events, and premium client service models.
 Actively contribute to shaping the future of the Private Client business in your market through an entrepreneurial, solution‑oriented mindset.

# ========================= Row 13 =====.title: Senior FP&A Analyst
# ========================= Row 13 =====.job_description:
This position sits in our Global Travel Retail business (GTR). GTR is one of the Group’s largest markets, bringing Pernod Ricard brands to life in the exciting and innovative Travel Retail channel, which includes airports, airlines & border shops around the globe. With a global footprint across more than 20 locations and over 30 nationalities, our working culture celebrates inclusion and diversity. Our team is united by our shared passion for travel and belief in its transformative power. Travel to the heart of our exciting business with our @pernodricardtravel Instagram page, showcasing our latest projects and innovations around the globe.

 It reports to FP&A Manager of Travel Retail Asia finance team. The job holder needs to:
 a) Contribute to optimize the company’s financial performance
 b) Control the accounting activities and validate the accounts
 c) Provide financial analysis on all activities, plans, targets and business drivers
 d) Ensure that company financial systems are robust, compliant and support current activities and future growth
 e) Ensure that the regulatory requirements are met

 Key Responsibilities / Accountabilities 

 Cash Reporting & Management: 
 Own the monthly/quarterly cash reporting process, ensuring timely and accurate submissions that meet HQ and GTR Cash/Treasury guidelines for rolling forecasts and R&O.
 Maintain clear visibility of cash movements by connecting changes in the P&L and Balance Sheet to the Cash Flow statement.
 Manage monthly closing activities, including Balance Sheet review, Overdue analysis, CAPEX tracking, and non-recurring item review, reporting figures accurately in the Group’s system.
 Oversee CAPEX management, including actual spend tracking and forecast planning
 Collaborate closely with the accounting team to review the Balance Sheet, understand variances, and ensure timely resolution.

 P&L Analysis & Management 
 Ensure timely and complete month-end closing and reporting in Group’s system, that comply with HQ guidelines.
 Analyze key P&L variances for actual results and quarterly rolling forecasts before submission.
 Support the annual Transfer Pricing process and validate the local P&L to ensure robust statutory reporting

 Business Partnering: 
 Provide exceptional business partnering to cross-functional teams (Commercial, Marketing, Operations, etc.) through financial modeling and KPI development, driving improved commercial outcomes.
 Engage effectively with stakeholders at various levels, including Headquarters, GTR management, and the Hong Kong Finance team.

 Ad-hoc projects 
 Support in any kind of any ad hoc analysis, presentations and projects as required.

 Qualifications/Requirements: 
 Bachelor’s degree in Accounting, Finance or related discipline.
 A minimum of 6-8 years’ FP&A experience gained from Multinational FMCG, or travel retail industries preferred; HKICPA, ACCA or CIMA qualification is a plus
 Ability to act as finance business partner with strong collaborative skillset and able to work with all levels across different functions effectively and efficiently
 Ability to adapt to a dynamic environment.
 Highly organized and detailed oriented
 Ability to work independently, strong result-orientated and eager to learn
 Strong analytical and problem-solving skills to provide good financial analysis
 Proficiency in Excel, PowerPoint, Power BI and reporting/visualization tools
 High proficiency in both spoken and written English
 Candidate with less experience will be considered as FP&A Analyst

 Job Posting End Date: 

 Target Hire Date : 
2025-12-31 

 Target End Date :
2026-01-01
